description_long: This object is a large, roughly rectangular stone block with a hollowed-out interior, resembling a trough or basin. The exterior surface is uneven and textured, with a natural, weathered stone appearance and visible chisel marks. The block is thick-walled, with a deep cavity that extends nearly the full length and width of the top face. The bottom edges are irregular, and there are some notches or cutouts along the base, suggesting it may have been designed to rest securely on uneven ground or to allow drainage. The coloration is a pale beige to light tan, typical of limestone or sandstone. There are no visible engravings or decorative features, giving it a utilitarian and ancient look.
description: A large, hollowed rectangular stone block with rough, weathered surfaces and a deep central cavity. Its pale, textured exterior and thick walls suggest durability and utilitarian use.
description_view_0: The block's open cavity and thick, rough front wall are clearly visible, with the stone's uneven texture prominent.
description_view_1: This side shows the notched base and more pronounced surface irregularities, with the hollow interior partially visible.
description_view_2: The opposite end of the block, showing a solid, rough face with the cavity opening visible at the top.
description_view_3: A rear view highlighting the notched lower edge and the block's overall rectangular form, with less of the cavity visible.
attribute_location_context: historic urban park or archaeological site
attribute_installation_method: set directly on compacted earth or stone base
attribute_weathering_pattern: Surface shows moderate weathering with rough, chipped edges and a generally pitted texture, indicating prolonged outdoor exposure.
